feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Identity Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Identity Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: High-skip Audit numbers in Audit table
entry.content:
Hi All, in IDM 8 there is a very high increase in Audit numbers in the mxp_audit table. i.e 100000 numbers have been skipped twice. This has happened after the Audit tables were cleaned up due to Audit overflow.

Please suggest as to why this skip has happened 

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Should Financial Platforms Assume User Understanding — or Technically Prove It Before Execution?
entry.content:
Across financial, enterprise, and transactional systems, most platforms still rely on a critical assumption:

That a user’s identity verification and acceptance of terms implies understanding.

From a systems-design perspective, this assumption feels increasingly fragile.

In high-impact workflows — payments, financial commitments, contract execution, irreversible actions — identity alone does not establish comprehension, intent, or informed consent. Yet most platforms treat it as sufficient.

My question is architectural, not philosophical:

• Should platforms remain passive recorders of identity and clicks?
• Or should they actively enforce verified comprehension before allowing execution?
• If so, where should that verification live — UI layer, middleware, or as a system-of-record?
• How would such a model integrate with existing enterprise identity, audit, and compliance frameworks?

I’m interested in how SAP architects and enterprise developers think about this tradeoff as systems move toward higher automation, AI-driven decisions, and irreversible digital actions.

Is “assumed understanding” still defensible at scale — or is it a design gap waiting to be formalized?

entry.title: Azure name and family name routing
entry.content:
Hi,

We have apps deployed to cloud foundy on a global account.
We have apps both in a space and in the HTML 5 repo.
We sometimes use a dedicated approuter and sometimes we deploy our own.

We have app users that connect using an Azure trust configuration in the subaccount.
We also have platform users using SAP's default IDP to login with an S-user.
This means that when a user wants to use an app or login to BTP cockpit he needs to choose which authentication to use,  SAP's default identity provider or the Azure connection.

I was asked to allow users to login without having to make this selection.
I setup and IAS trust configuration and inside the IAS I defined the Azure IDP and routing based on email hostname.
Now users are greated by the IAS login and when they input their azure email it redirects them to login using azure and when platform users use their email they login directly through IAS versus the users' creds I defined in the IAS.

The problem:
All our apps get data about the currently logged in user(to the app) via the approuter's /userinfo.
Now after setting up the IAS and the routing, if i log in as an Azure user i see in the devtools im not getting name and family name in the /userinfo any more, only email information.
I tried all orts of manipulations in the IAS but i cant see to get it to display name and family name like it does with a direct azure trust configuration.
Is there any IAS configuration solution to this issue or must we modify all our apps?

entry.title: Best Practice for Managing Business Role Changes After an Upgrade
entry.content:
Dear community,

what is in your opinion the best way to manage the changes after an upgrade in consideration of transports of software collections?

At the moment it seems for me, you have to maintain the changes two times with a 3 landscape system:

1. Update the business roles in Test system to make the changes available for testing the release

2. Update the business roles in Q system again after the release was deploy to Q and Productive System to transport the changes with the software collection to all 3 systems

Is this the correct procedure? Is there a better way which I am missing?

thank you for your inputs, 

kind regards

Regina

entry.title: IAS and Onboarding
entry.content:
Hi there

Is there anywhere in SAP documentation that it mentions IAS set up is mandatory for ONB? Or the best practice approach is to enable IAS for Onboarding authentication for external users?

SAP support have provided advice that it is NOT recommended to enable IAS for ONB, which I thought was mandatory for ONB? @Gopinath_Murugaiyan are you able to confirm what is best practice when it comes to ONB and IAS and login method for external hires? Should IAS be enabled for onboarding or should it not (i.e. utilise the standard email in SF ONB Email Services)?

Thanks

entry.title: IAS activation email not triggered after 2nd rehire check
entry.content:
Hello all, 

Has anyone come across IAS issues with welcome email not being triggered after 2nd rehire check and ONB is restarted for the new hire? The SF standard ONB emails get triggered, but there is no new IAS welcome email to activate their account after the rehire match. As per this KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3585126/E, there should be a 2nd activation email or password reset email of some sort triggered from IAS, but this isn't happening for the client.

I have a High priority ticket with SAP and it's been with their engineering team for a week now with no update, hoping someone here has an answer or can at least point me in the right direction.

Thank you!

entry.title: Adding Learning Content Provider as Application in IAS
entry.content:
Hello,

We are in the process of setting up a new learning content provider in our SF LMS system.  For various reasons for authentication we have been told that the best route to take would be to set the learning content provider up as an application in IAS. 

We are an SSO environment and have SSO configured in IAS.  By setting up the Learning Content Provider in IAS, it would use IAS for authentication when a person is accessing a course.  

I logged a ticket with SAP and I was given this KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0002677737 and told that this would be the correct path to take and that we do not need to configure it in IAS. 

I went back to the Learning Content Provider with this information. They have said that while this works, it is very basic and will only send the userID and not information like names and that other customers of theirs have set them up in IAS. 

Just curious if anyone has had this scenario before and what approach they took? 

Kind regards,
Helen

entry.title: Account privilege of a Business role in mcexecstate 1028
entry.content:
Hi All,

idm 8.2

kindly advise as direct removal and assignment of the failed(execstate =1028) Account privilege has not succeeded and the Account privilege stays in 1028.

this privilege was assigned through a business role and therefore the business role appears as Failed. Since other privileges of the same business role have succeeded, hence it is not desired to have the business role removed.

Please suggest of there is any other approach other than manual database adjustment of the execstate

note: repair job has failed to remove.

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Transactions in identity lifecycle management
entry.content:
Hi

I am new to SAP, and I have a question re this picture in one of the courses:

The best practice in cryptography is to rely on stateless workflows. That is, at the time of authentication an identity provider would issue e.g., a JWT with all authorizations for resources the user is entitled to. The token could then be used in OAuth/ zero-trust environment for transparent access to the SAP cloud on the right-hand side. I would imagine the green arrow "Identity Lifecycle Management" only injects public keys a JWT would be verified against. But because those keys are public, they would usually be pulled from PKI whenever needed, so the push model (injection) is superfluous. I suspect my understanding could be wrong - if someone could please clarify? Is this authentication/authorization workflow a common place among Cloud foundry, ABAP, and Kyma? Maybe you could point me out to a good online course for this?

entry.title: Cloud ALM User
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on SAP Cloud ALM setup and I am not sure what the best way is to onboard users to SAP Cloud ALM.

My setup:

- Corporate IDP
- IAS as Proxy
- Users are provisioned from the corporate IdP into IAS

Now I am unsure what the best method is for Cloud ALM user onboarding.

As far as I understand, there are these options:
1. CALM User Management App (manual user creation in Cloud ALM)
2. BTP Role Collections (central assignment via BTP Cockpit)
3. IPS group based provisioning

My questions:

What is the best practice in real projects?
What is most commonly used in production?
Are there any known issues or limitations with these approaches?

Thanks for your help!!!

Best Regards

entry.title: IAS App-to-App Navigation Failing: UI5 AppRouter → CAP Node.js via Destination
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I’m trying to implement SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS / AMS) based authentication between two separately deployed BTP applications (MTAs), but the token exchange from UI5 AppRouter to CAP backend keeps failing.

I’d appreciate guidance on whether my architecture is valid, and what configuration I may have missed.

Landscape

Application A (Consumer)

UI5 frontend with standalone AppRouter

  • Deployed in BTP Cloud Foundry

  • Uses IAS login

  • Calls backend via Destination Service

Application B (Provider)

CAP Node.js service

  • Separate MTA

  • Protected by IAS / AMS

  • Has provided-apis configured

Expected Flow:

User
→ UI5 AppRouter
→ Destination (IASDependencyName)
→ AppRouter exchanges token
→ CAP backend receives exchanged token
→ CAP authorizes user

Current Destination Setup

Name: CAP_AMS_BACKEND
Type: HTTP
Authentication: NoAuthentication
URL: https://<cap-service-url>

Additional Properties:
HTML5.IASDependencyName = cap-ams //setup in IAS

I have also tried with destination type = oAuth2UserTokenExchange but got the same 401 error. Error screenshot below. Looks like the destination service client Id is the caller to IAS for token exchange but IAS is rejecting the audience.

UI5 App xs-app.json

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/$1",
      "destination": "CAP_AMS_BACKEND",
      "authenticationType": "ias"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "localDir": "resources",
      "authenticationType": "ias"
    }
  ]
}

 CAP App ias instance

UI5 App ias instance

 

Dependency set on Consumer --> Provider App in IAS

All the grant types are selected in the provider and the consumer app for testing

 
What I Want to Confirm:
  1. Is this architecture officially supported? Standalone UI5 AppRouter (MTA A) calling separate CAP Node.js app (MTA B) via destination using IAS App-to-App navigation?
  2. Should this work using only:
    Authentication = NoAuthentication / oAuth2UserTokenExchange
    HTML5.IASDependencyName = <dependencyName>
  3. Should both apps share the same IAS instance, or is separate IAS instance + dependency the correct model?
  4. Has anyone implemented this successfully with CAP Node.js?

Thanks in advance,

Deepak

entry.title: Default SAP Identity Provider (IdP) Behavior for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Hello!

I would like to get a better understanding on how the default SAP Identity Provider (IdP) works for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

We are currently facing an issue with SSO for SAP Analytics Cloud, and we have reverted to the default IdP from a custom IdP. Since then I am not able to log in to SAP Analytics Cloud (with my SAP credentials) and I get the 'It seems that you don't have an active account' page. 

I have verified my account with https://hanacloudservices-eu.accounts.ondemand.com/ and have also reset my password. The credentials I set during the authentication are aligned with the details displayed in Profile Management (https://hanacloudservices-eu.accounts.ondemand.com/).

It also seems that some users are able to log in without any issues using their SAP credentials.

Any insights into how the default SAP IdP for SAP Analytics Cloud functions and what might be causing this issue would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks, and best regards,

CS

entry.title: SAP IDM Admin UI not working due to Service is down error.
entry.content:
HI Gurus

 

We cant access IDM Admin UI it gives us the Access Denied, Service is Down error. I went and tried to ping a connection in the Datasource in IDM /NWA and got an error. I have looked everywhere and i suspect that the problem is 

 

I have asked Basis to look for these Admin and Provisioning users for both data sources in Linux. Once they find them, they can reset their passwords. However, they are not winning in finding them. It looks like they were deleted.  If they were deleted, should we recreate them . Where else can we look? Eclipse is also not working.

entry.title: Cloud Identity Services Trial Admin Console Returns forbidden.html
entry.content:
I created a new SAP BTP Trial account and subscribed to Cloud Identity Services (default plan).

The tenant was created successfully and I can log in. Profile Management works correctly.

However, when I open the Administration Console (either from BTP Cockpit → Go to Application or directly via https://.trial-accounts.ondemand.com/admin), I am redirected to:

/admin/forbidden.html

Additional information:

  • User has Subaccount Administrator role.

  • Trust Configuration is healthy.

  • Administration Console application is shown as ACTIVE for the user.

  • Profile Management works.

  • Trial account was created on June 10, 2026.

Expected result:
Administration Console opens successfully.

Actual result:
forbidden.html is displayed.

Has anyone seen this with a newly created trial tenant, and is there a way to repair the administrator assignment without recreating the tenant?

entry.title: Unable to Access Cloud Identity Services Admin Console – "You are not authorized to view the content
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am using an SAP BTP Trial account and have recently set up Cloud Identity Services.

The steps I completed are:

Created an SAP BTP Trial account and subaccount.
Enabled Cloud Identity Services from the BTP cockpit.
Established trust between the BTP subaccount and Cloud Identity Services.
Received the activation email and successfully activated the Cloud Identity Services tenant using the activation link.

Issue: After activation, when I am redirected to the Cloud Identity Services administration portal, I receive the following error message:

"You are not authorized to view the content. Please contact your administrator."

In some cases, the page also displays a "Forbidden" error.

The authentication process itself appears to complete successfully, and I can log in with my credentials. However, after authentication, I am unable to access the administration console.

What is confusing is that I am the administrator who activated the tenant from the activation email, so I expected to have administrative access to the Cloud Identity Services tenant.

Additional Information:

Environment: SAP BTP Trial
Cloud Identity Services tenant activated successfully
Trust configuration completed in BTP
Issue occurs immediately after authentication and redirection to the admin console
Has anyone experienced a similar issue with a trial account? Is there an additional role assignment or configuration step required after tenant activation?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: Unexpected Connections Consumption in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently implementing a Supplier Portal using SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

Our current subscription includes:

1,000 Active Users
0 Connections

Authentication is performed through SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication - IAS) as our Identity Provider (IdP).

Due to an omission during the commercial contracting process, our subscription was provisioned with Active Users only, while the Connections metric was not included. We are already discussing this situation with our SAP Account Team, but we would like to better understand both the technical behavior and whether anyone has experienced a similar situation.

During our testing, we observed that every external login generates Connections, even though our subscription does not include this metric.

From a technical perspective, we would like to know:

1. Is the generation of the Connections metric mandatory in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition?
2. Is there any way to completely prevent the generation of the Connections metric through Role Collections, authorizations or SAP Build Work Zone configuration?

From a commercial perspective, we would also appreciate guidance from anyone who has experienced a similar situation.

Any guidance or shared experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: How to find out users mapped to SAC ID
entry.content: We had an issue in production and had to find out list of users who accessed story reports. With story report performance tool was able to find out how many users opened story reports in a given window. However, the list only has SAC IDs of users. We tried expert chat, checking IAS mapping and employee export but couldn't figure out actual users using SAC IDs. Can someone help please?

entry.title: SAP IAS connection to SAP BTP via OIDC is not working
entry.content:
I have established the connectivity between IAS and BTP using OIDC via the "establish trust" button in trust configuration. All the necessary configuration is also done. ROle collection is also provided under trust configuration.

However, when I am assigning the user in IAS, it says missing authorization while logging to BTP. 

It is not creating the use in the subaccount as well. 

Any suggestions please? 

I can go for SAML but Joule enablement needs only OIDC. Hence I am stuck.

entry.title: Request to Enable Onboarding SSO with a Single Email Notification from Onboarding Email Services
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have received a business requirement to enable Single Sign-On (SSO) for candidates during the Onboarding process.

The expectation is that only one email notification should be triggered from Onboarding Email Services, and this email should include the SSO login link for the candidate. No additional or separate system-generated emails should be sent as part of the onboarding initiation process.

Could you please confirm:

  1. Whether this configuration is supported in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding.
  2. If supported, the required settings and configuration steps to ensure that only a single onboarding email is sent containing the SSO link.
  3. Any prerequisites, limitations, or best practices associated with this setup.
  4. Whether there are any known impacts on candidate access, authentication, or onboarding workflow communications.

We would appreciate your guidance on achieving this requirement and any relevant documentation or KBAs that can assist with the configuration.

Regards,

Swapna

entry.title: User not as Member of Role after provisioning IAS to AS ABAP
entry.content:
Hi team,

 

we are currently testing user provisioning to AS ABAP following Docu: SAP Application Server ABAP | SAP Help Portal 

The connection succeeded and we were able to update user Attributes for a testuser.

We are testing the Role assignment with Group "ZS_SR_X_X_0257" wich is representative in CIS and our AS ABAP.

The testuser is assigned to this group in CIS, but wont get assigned in AS ABAP after a read or sync Job. 

The technical user has SAP_ALL so it wont be authorization issues. We can also see that the user provisioning is deleting all users wich were already assigned to that group in AS ABAP when we set parameter "ips.overwrite.existedbefore.assignments =true". So the groups is detected and can be edited.

 

Regarding to target system transformation (default from Docu) the groups are set as following:

{
                "sourcePath": "$.members[*].value",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.USERLIST[?(@.USERNAME)]"
            }

In the provisioning log we can see that the system is updating the correct role "ZS_SR_X_X_0257" and user "XY"

"group:ZS_SR_X_X_0257,
system=SC2,
content={"ROLE_NAME":"ZS_SR_X_X_0257","USERLIST":[{"USERNAME":"XY"}]}"

Greetings
Rouven
